phrase.text: When <odds and ends|bits and pieces> of life on land - particularly plastics - end up in the sea, they pose <hazards| threats> to marine life.
Animals drown or strangle from becoming <tangled|caught> in discarded or lost fishing gear.
Needles and <syringes|hypodermic>, washed from city streets and sewer systems after heavy rain storms, can wash up on beaches.
Estimated costs from lost tourism and <recreation|leisure> can run into billions.
Biologists who performed an autopsy on an <emaciated|thin> male sperm whale <beached|grounded> at Sea Side Heights, New Jersey, found a party balloon.
Ducie Atoll is one of the most <remote|isolated> islands in the world.
An important breeding area for seabirds, the Ducie Atoll is <littered|covered> with trash.
